Title: Neophytadiene, a Plant Specialized Metabolite, Mediates the Virus-Vector-Plant Tripartite Interactions

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes chlorophyll degradation and strongly increases neophytadiene production, thereby enhancing whitefly attraction to infected plants
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Putative 9 kDa tomato chlorosis virus protein; unique to Crinivirus
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Volatile attractant that promotes Bemisia tabaci preference and facilitates virus transmission
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ToCV infection; transient overexpression of viral proteins in tobacco and tomato
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for antiviral engineering to disrupt virus-induced volatile manipulation and reduce vector attraction
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increases chlorophyll loss, chloroplast damage, and likely reduces photosynthetic performance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among 11 ToCV proteins, P9 was the only protein whose overexpression specifically increased neophytadiene accumulation. 2. P9 overexpression in tomato increased neophytadiene 8.4-fold and reduced chlorophyll by 54%. 3. P9 physically interacted with host Lhca4 and this was linked to PSI/LHCI disruption, chlorophyll degradation, and increased whitefly preference.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PVX pGR106 transient expression system; 35S-based constructs for interaction assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient overexpression; metabolite profiling; whitefly preference assay; yeast two-hybrid; Co-IP; luciferase complementation; BiFC; co-localization; BN-PAGE; comparative proteomics
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts in tomato and Nicotiana benthamiana as a viral effector linking host chlorophyll breakdown to volatile-mediated vector behavior
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Viral protein expressed during ToCV infection

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Maintains chlorophyll accumulation and suppresses neophytadiene production; overexpression reduces whitefly attraction and ToCV impact
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Light-harvesting complex I subunit; photosystem I antenna protein
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neophytadiene functions as a volatile attractant for whiteflies and promotes virus spread
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ToCV infection; P9 expression; transgenic overexpression; CRISPR knockout
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful host target for engineering virus-resistant, low-attractiveness tomato lines with lower neophytadiene release
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of Lhca4 shortens growth period and causes stunted seeds; overexpression altered plant height and chlorophyll traits
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Lhca4 physically interacted with ToCV P9. 2. Lhca4 overexpression increased chlorophyll content by 37% and reduced neophytadiene by 80%, while knockout had the opposite effect. 3. Overexpression alleviated ToCV/P9-induced chlorosis, reduced whitefly preference, and lowered neophytadiene after infection.
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pBWA(V)HS-osgfp overexpression vector; 35S-based FLAG/mCherry/nLUC constructs in transient assays
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; chlorophyll measurement; neophytadiene profiling; whitefly preference assay; Y2H; Co-IP; LCI; BiFC; co-localization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ToCV infection suppressed Lhca4 expression; Lhca4 overexpression induced chlorophyll synthesis genes CHLH, CHLM, and POR and reduced chlorophyll catabolic gene responses under infection
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced upon ToCV infection; elevated in overexpression lines

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Binds neophytadiene and mediates whitefly preference for ToCV-infected plants, promoting virus transmission
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Acts as an attractant cue for vector host choice and increases viral spread
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Neophytadiene fumigation; dsRNA-mediated silencing; ToCV transmission assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for vector-interference strategies to block perception of neophytadiene and reduce virus spread
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BtOBP2 expression increased during neophytadiene exposure more strongly than other tested OBPs. 2. Recombinant BtOBP2 bound neophytadiene with the strongest affinity among tested volatiles (Ki 2.02 μM). 3. Silencing BtOBP2 abolished preference for neophytadiene and reduced ToCV accumulation after vector-mediated transmission.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: AlphaFold2 structural modeling; molecular docking; accession NA
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Soluble olfactory protein in sensillar lymph; extracellular
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression profiling under volatile exposure; RNA interference; Y-tube olfactometer assay; recombinant protein purification; fluorescence competitive binding assay; molecular docking
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BtOBP3 also responded transcriptionally to neophytadiene but silencing BtOBP3 did not abolish attraction, supporting BtOBP2 as the decisive OBP
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced over 0–96 h by neophytadiene exposure
